item.title: tool-review-notion
item.description: Notion AI provides a one-time trial of 20 complimentary responses per workspace for users on the Free or Plus plans.

item.title: garden_roadmap
item.description: Garden Content Roadmap and Site Design Notes hris28.github.io/garden — Planning Document Working reference for content, structure, writing principles, and design decisions.

item.title: Browser Fingerprinting
item.description: What browser fingerprinting collects, how unique your browser's fingerprint is, and what defenses actually work.

item.title: What Your ISP Can See
item.description: A layer-by-layer account of what your Internet Service Provider can observe, under what conditions, and what actually changes when you use HTTPS, encrypted DNS, or a VPN.

item.title: Software Layers: From Firmware to Application
item.description: The eleven layers between your hardware and your applications, what each one does, and why the distinctions matter for security and performance.

item.title: Tor Explained
item.description: How Tor routes traffic, why it is different from a VPN, its real limits, and when to use it.

item.title: Garden Design Notes
item.description: Design patterns for articles that include live demonstrations, structured comparisons, and layered technical explanations.

item.title: Tool Review: Bitwarden
item.description: Bitwarden is the most commonly recommended open-source password manager. Here is what it actually does, how its encryption model works, and why the open-source and self-hosting options matter.

item.title: Tool Review: DuckDuckGo
item.description: DuckDuckGo is the most commonly recommended private search engine. Here is what it actually protects, what it does not, and what its business model means for the user.

item.title: Tool Review: Mozilla Firefox
item.description: Firefox is the only major browser not controlled by an advertising company. Here is what that means in practice, what it does well, and what configuration actually changes.
